steps.title: Identify the claim to refute
steps.content: The student wants to refute the claim that "malapportionment in the Storting favors small urban districts." To refute it, the answer must show the opposite—that malapportionment actually favors rural districts.

steps.title: Locate the supporting note
steps.content: The notes state seats are awarded by formula at 1.8 points per unit of land vs. 1 point per resident, and that "less populated rural districts with large tracts of land receive a disproportionate number of seats." Citing this directly refutes the urban-favoring claim.

steps.title: Eliminate by misread/overreach
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'rural districts are disproportionally underrepresented... unfair advantage for... urban districts': contradicts the notes (rural are over-, not under-represented) and reinforces the claim instead of refuting it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'formula... overrepresents more populated districts': also contradicts the notes—less populated rural districts are the ones overrepresented. ✗
  • Choice C — 'A common feature of representative governments...': just defines malapportionment without addressing who benefits in Norway. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Awarding more points per unit of land than... per resident... overrepresents less populated rural districts': cites the exact land-vs-resident weighting and identifies rural overrepresentation, directly refuting the urban-favoring claim. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
